``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} # Parole Eligibility ## How Parole Eligibility is Determined For maximum terms no longer than four years, prison sentences have no parole release eligibility. Maximum terms of 20 years or more almost always include parole release eligibility with reductions for good time credits; this group of people in prison becomes eligible for discretionary parole release after serving one-half of their judicial minimum terms. This means that no matter how long a minimum term the judge may have selected and because the minimum may not exceed the maximum first parole eligibility will always come at the 50-percent mark of the judicial maximum sentence or earlier.
## In Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Year In 2020, there were 29 people in prison past their parole eligibility year. This group made up 1% of people in prison for new crimes and with sentence lengths between 1 to 25 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Demographics The demographics of people in prison past their parole eligibility year reveal notable proportions among Black, non-Hispanic and white, non-hispanic people. Gender distribution data is incomplete or missing. Age-wise, the majority of people were 35-44 years and 25-34 years old. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Criminal Offenses ::: {.cell} ::: In 2020, 52% of people in prison past their parole eligibility year were in prison for non-violent offenses. The breakdown of criminal offenses of people in prison past their parole eligibility year reveals a varied landscape, with the majority of people incarcerated for public order (24%) and robbery (17%) offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Original Sentence Lengths In 2020, among the prison population eligible for parole but not yet released, people with sentences between 10 to 24.9 years constituted the majority, representing 93%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Missing Data This section will contain information related to missingness in the NCRP data. Keep?


# Prison Population ## Prison Population Trends From 2010 to 2022, the prison population increased 22%, changing from 4,587 in 2010 to 5,600 in 2022. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners Series
## Demographics ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners 2022 - Race and Ethnicity

``` ::: :::
## Sentence Lengths ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Prison Releases ## Prison Release Trends From 2010 to 2020, prison releases increased 6%, dropping from 2,304 in 2010 to 2,432 in 2020. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
In 2020, 48% of people eligible for parole were released during their eligibility year. This represents a 11% decrease compared to 2010. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Conditional Releases Conditional release involves an individual’s release under specific conditions and supervision, whereas unconditional release means the individual is released without further obligations or restrictions. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Demographics of People Released The demographics of people released from prison reveal notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and black, non-hispanic people. Gender distribution indicates a predominance of males over females. Age-wise, the majority of people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. These findings provide insights into the populations transitioning back into the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Time Served by Offense Between 2010 and 2020, there were shifts in the average time served by individuals for different offense types in Nebraska. The largest change was for other or unknown offenses, which increased by 1633%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Disparities ## Incarceration Rates In Nebraska, Black, non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 9.4 times higher and Hispanic, any race people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 2.3 times higher and Other race(s), non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 2.9 times higher than White, non-Hispanic people, when accounting for population sizes in the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Years Spent in Prison After Parole Eligibility ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity In 2020, people in prison past their parole eligibility year collectively spent a total of 37 years behind bars instead of supervised in the community on parole. This extended incarceration varied across different racial and ethnic groups.

## Timing of Release After Parole Eligibility ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity The timing of release for individuals after their parole eligibility year varied by offense type and demographic group, with many remaining in prison for several years beyond their eligibility date. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender :::
## Time Served ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity ::: {.cell} ::: Black, non-Hispanic individuals faced the longest average time served in prison in 2020, with an average of 2.2 years. Other race(s), non-Hispanic individuals experienced shorter prison stays, averaging 1.8 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: By offense type, disparities were observed in time served by race and ethnicity. For Rape or Sexual Assault offenses, Other race(s), non-Hispanic individuals had 3.01 more years on average compared to Hispanic, any race individuals, who had the shortest time served for these offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
